title: Basic Genealogy

jokes.text: Oh, speaking of, I was going to invite my mom so you could meet her, but, uh, she still thinks I'm a lawyer.
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions inviting his mom to meet someone
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's lying to his mother about his career
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff - maintaining a false image even to his mother. Perfect character integration showing his vanity and insecurity.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I invited my mom to meet someone.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I've been telling her I'm a successful lawyer.

jokes.text: The human being has a little human with it-- How blood curdlingly adorable.
jokes.setup: Seeing someone with a child at family day
jokes.punchline: Describing it as 'blood curdlingly adorable' - mixing horror with cute
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of typical 'adorable' reaction with horror movie language. Very economical and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's blood curdlingly adorable.

jokes.text: What's wrong? Are you breaking up with me? Oh. [giggles] Maybe we don't need to talk.
jokes.setup: Someone says 'We need to talk' - universally ominous
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate assumption it's a breakup, then relief when it's not
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's relationship paranoia and quick relief.

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: We need to talk.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh god. Is this a breakup? Are you breaking up with me?

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: What? No.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh thank god.

jokes.text: Hope they're not twins. Twins freak me out. They always know what the other one is-- Thinking? Yeah. And they're always finishing each other's-- Pie. Exactly. It's creepy.
jokes.setup: Someone explaining their fear of twins with the classic 'finishing each other's sentences' setup
jokes.punchline: Instead of 'sentences,' they say 'pie' - completely random
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of a tired trope. The unexpected 'pie' completely derails the expected 'sentences' and the other person's 'Exactly!' response makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I have this weird fear of twins, you know? Like, they finish each other's—

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Pie.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: They finish each other's pie. That's what scared you?

jokes.text: My sister likes to joke that they don't recognize me anymore, and I like to joke that she's just jealous because she's barren.
jokes.setup: Sister making a harmless joke about Shirley's kids not recognizing her
jokes.punchline: Shirley's brutal counter-joke about her sister being barren
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley - sweet on the surface but savage underneath. The contrast between harmless teasing and brutal retaliation is excellent character work.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: At least my kids recognize me. Your eggs expired so long ago, they need carbon dating.

jokes.text: Does she look like you but in a wig and lipstick? No, that's Halle Berry.
jokes.setup: Question about what someone's female cousin looks like
jokes.punchline: Abed's deadpan correction that Halle Berry is the one who looks like him in drag
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed deadpan delivery with unexpected pop culture reference that actually makes sense in context.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: What does your female cousin look like?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Halle Berry.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: No. Halle Berry looks like me in drag.

jokes.text: You cherish her. I'm sure I will. You know she's not gonna be around forever. I wanna believe you're right, Britta. But you never quite are, are you?
jokes.setup: Britta giving earnest advice about cherishing elderly relatives
jokes.punchline: Troy's polite but cutting observation that Britta is always wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics - Britta's earnestness met with Troy's innocent but devastating honesty. Great callback to ongoing character trait.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You really need to cherish your elderly relatives while you still have them. Life is short, and you never know when they'll be gone.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's good advice, Britta. You're wrong about literally everything else, but you're right about this.

jokes.text: I'll give Jeff a shoulder to cry on, and you boys go put a dead bird in that bitch's glove compartment.
jokes.setup: Offering comfort to Jeff after his breakup
jokes.punchline: Immediately segueing into revenge plans involving a dead bird
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Shirley moment - motherly comfort immediately followed by shockingly specific revenge fantasy. The contrast is perfect and the specificity of 'dead bird' makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh honey, I know you're hurting right now, but you're going to get through this. I'm here for you.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know what? We're going to get revenge. I've got a dead bird in my freezer from last week, and I know exactly where she parks her car.

jokes.text: Your heart's desensitized, Jeff. Like a machine, it feels no love--just sex. You're like the booty-nator.
jokes.setup: Troy comparing Jeff to a machine that only wants sex
jokes.punchline: Creating the portmanteau 'booty-nator' - Terminator + booty
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent attempt at being clever with wordplay. The earnestness makes it funnier than the joke itself.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You're like a machine that only wants one thing.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: The Booty-nator.

jokes.text: Why would the booty-nator be back with booty? Wouldn't he just try and kill it? Well, maybe he kills it and then brings it back as a trophy. Why would you want dead booty? I want pirate's booty. Because you're hungry?
jokes.setup: Troy's 'booty-nator' concept being logically examined
jokes.punchline: Escalating confusion ending with Pierce wanting pirate's booty snacks
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of absurdity as the group tries to make sense of Troy's nonsensical concept, ending with Pierce's practical hunger.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: So your 'booty-nator' detects bootys within a five-mile radius?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Exactly! It's foolproof.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: But how does it differentiate between a human booty and, say, a donkey?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: It... it just knows.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Does it work on pirates?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Why would pirates—

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Because I want some booty. The snack mix. From the store.

jokes.text: Well, you're too gorgeous to be a teacher, and you're too happy to be a student.
jokes.setup: Jeff hitting on someone at family day
jokes.punchline: His pickup line about community college demographics
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff pickup line that's both smooth and revealing about community college culture. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what they say about community college girls... the odds are ever in your favor. Statistically speaking, there are more women here than men, which means mathematically, I'm basically a celebrity.

jokes.text: No, actually, it's a very big world with five billion other women in it.
jokes.setup: Pierce commenting on what a small world it is that Jeff knows Amber
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact correction emphasizing he has other options
jokes.explanation: Jeff's quick retreat showing his discomfort with the Pierce connection. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It's a small world.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's not that small. I know lots of girls.

jokes.text: I'm back. Without booty. Now I feel sorry for you.
jokes.setup: Jeff returns from failed pickup attempt
jokes.punchline: Annie's faux sympathy for his failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure with Annie's sarcastic response to Jeff's failure fitting her character's judgmental side.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh no, what happened? Did she reject you?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: She didn't reject me. She just... wasn't interested.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Right, so she rejected you. That's so sad. Do you want me to call you a cab?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not drunk.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: No, a cab to take you home. You look terrible.

jokes.text: I'm back. Without booty. Now I feel sorry for you.
jokes.setup: Jeff returns after striking out with Amber
jokes.punchline: Annie's pity confirms his failed 'booty-nator' mission
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that works but lacks impact. Functional but forgettable.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So how'd it go with Amber?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh Jeff, I'm so sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That bad, huh? The booty-nator strikes again.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You'll get 'em next time.

jokes.text: Orange alert! Sorry, they're just excited.
jokes.setup: Kids playing some kind of security game
jokes.punchline: Using Homeland Security alert levels as play dialogue
jokes.explanation: Kids incorporating post-9/11 security culture into their play. Mildly funny social commentary.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Okay, kids, we're playing a security game. I'm the Homeland Security officer.

dialogue.speaker: Elijah
dialogue.line: What level are we at?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: We're at orange. That means be alert.

dialogue.speaker: Jordan
dialogue.line: What about red?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Red means run around screaming.

dialogue.speaker: Elijah
dialogue.line: We're at red.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: No, we're not!

dialogue.speaker: Jordan
dialogue.line: We're at red.

jokes.text: And that's Elijah, like the prophet, and Jordan, like the 14 time NBA all-star.
jokes.setup: Introducing her children with name explanations
jokes.punchline: Mixing biblical reference with sports statistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley - mixing religious and secular references naturally. Shows her character's cultural duality.

jokes.text: Is that how you say hello? That's my name. Oh! I'm sorry. You're like a black ghost.
jokes.setup: Shirley misunderstanding Abed's father's name as a greeting
jokes.punchline: Her awkward racial comment about him being 'like a black ghost'
jokes.explanation: Shirley's well-meaning but awkward attempt to recover from cultural confusion. Typical cringe moment.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh my God, hello Gubi! I'm so happy to meet you!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's not a greeting, Shirley. That's my father's name.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh! I'm so sorry. You're so... you're like a black ghost.

jokes.text: Over the course of seven marriages, I've amassed some 32 stepchildren. And obviously, Amber's my favorite.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining his complicated family situation
jokes.punchline: The absurd numbers (7 marriages, 32 stepchildren) and casual favoritism
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - absurdly wealthy lifestyle with inappropriate oversharing. The numbers are ridiculous enough to be funny.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I've been married seven times. I have thirty-two stepchildren. I can't remember all their names, so I just call the ones I like 'sport' and the rest of them 'buddy.'

jokes.text: But judging from that bluetooth headset, his son is. Cha-ching!
jokes.setup: Seeing Star-Burns isn't a virgin
jokes.punchline: But his son with a bluetooth headset definitely is
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor linking bluetooth headsets to virginity. Good setup/payoff structure.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: But his son with a bluetooth headset definitely is

jokes.text: Everyone here is my family. [Everyone boos] Weak.
jokes.setup: Jeff gives a sentimental answer about the study group being his family
jokes.punchline: The group immediately rejects this sentiment as weak
jokes.explanation: Good group dynamic moment - Jeff's attempt at sincerity is immediately shut down. The booing is harsh but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You guys are my family. My real family.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That's the most pathetic thing I've ever heard.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, dude, that's sad.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Your delivery was bad and you should feel bad.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We're not your family, Jeff. We're your friends. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: And frankly, that comparison is insulting to families everywhere.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I've heard better sentiments from a Hallmark card written by a depressed robot.

jokes.text: And it's none of your business, but I ate my twin in utero.
jokes.setup: Chang being defensive about his family situation
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he absorbed his twin in the womb
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang - inappropriate overshare delivered with casual defensiveness. Extremely quotable and memorable dark humor.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Look, my family situation is complicated, okay? My twin brother and I, we had a very close relationship in the womb. In fact, you could say I absorbed him. Literally. I'm not proud of it, but it happened.

jokes.text: The call girls I frequent are not nearly as attractive as my daughter. Stepdaughter. Technically ex-stepdaughter.
jokes.setup: Pierce defending himself against assumptions about escorts
jokes.punchline: Comparing prostitutes to his stepdaughter, then correcting the relationship status
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Pierce moment - inappropriate comparison followed by awkward relationship clarifications.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm not saying I've never paid for sex. I'm saying the women I pay for sex are not prostitutes.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: They're more like my stepdaughter.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, they were my stepdaughter. We got divorced.

jokes.text: You want me to wingman you with your ex-stepdaughter?
jokes.setup: Pierce asking for help with Amber
jokes.punchline: Jeff's incredulous summary of the inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat where Jeff highlights the absurdity of Pierce's request through repetition.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So you want me to seduce your girlfriend so you can break up with her?

jokes.text: Disappointing you is like choking the little mermaid with a bike chain.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing Annie's doe eyes and how hard she is to disappoint
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and dark metaphor mixing Disney with violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jeff line - highly original metaphor that's both darkly funny and perfectly captures the sentiment. Extremely quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's like those doe eyes of hers are Bambi, and I'm the hunter, and I just shot his mother. Except the hunter feels worse than the deer.

jokes.text: Tell me, how many men do you think I've laid with?
jokes.setup: Britta awkwardly trying to compliment Troy's grandmother
jokes.punchline: Nana's direct and inappropriate sexual question
jokes.explanation: Shocking elderly character subversion - grandmother asking inappropriate sexual questions. Good character establishment.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Your skin is so... smooth. Do you use a special moisturizer?

dialogue.speaker: Nana
dialogue.line: Why, thank you dear. I use cocoa butter. Say, how often do you have sex?

jokes.text: A switch is a stick she can use to whup you with.
jokes.setup: Britta not knowing what a switch is
jokes.punchline: Troy's matter-of-fact explanation of corporal punishment
jokes.explanation: Cultural humor - white Britta's ignorance of traditional punishment methods. Troy's casual delivery helps.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: A switch is a thin stick or branch used for hitting people, usually as punishment. It's not a light switch, Britta.

jokes.text: Cherish, Britta. Cherish.
jokes.setup: Britta about to get physically disciplined by his grandmother
jokes.punchline: Troy sarcastically reminding her of her earlier advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback timing - Troy throwing Britta's own words back at her as she faces the consequences. Great economy and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Remember when you said, 'I'm not afraid of your grandmother'? Yeah, that was adorable.

jokes.text: Señor Chang? Yeah. Ben, do you realize how ridiculous that sounds? Okay, that is who I am. So drop it!
jokes.setup: Chang's brother questioning his name choice
jokes.punchline: Chang's defensive assertion of his ridiculous persona
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chang's awareness but commitment to his absurd persona.

dialogue.speaker: Chang's brother
dialogue.line: Why would you call yourself 'Ben Chang'? That's just your name.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: It's not just my name, okay? It's a BRAND. Ben Chang is a man of mystery, a man of intrigue, a man who—

dialogue.speaker: Chang's brother
dialogue.line: You're a community college student.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: A MYSTERIOUS community college student.

jokes.text: They're like Islamic turtlenecks.
jokes.setup: Explaining burkas to children
jokes.punchline: Comparing religious garments to casual clothing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected comparison that's both innocent and absurd. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: A burka is a religious garment worn by some Muslim women. It's like how some of you wear hoodies – it's just what they choose to wear for their faith.

jokes.text: Now she says he's a pig anus.
jokes.setup: Translating heated Arabic argument between father and cousin
jokes.punchline: Deadpan delivery of increasingly personal insults
jokes.explanation: Abed's matter-of-fact translation of family drama, escalating from mild disagreement to specific anatomical insults.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My father says your mother's cooking is worse than the food at the airport.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My cousin says at least his mother doesn't dress like she's still living in 1987.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My father says that's rich coming from someone who still uses a flip phone.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My cousin says he uses a flip phone because at least he has something that actually closes, unlike your father's mouth.

jokes.text: Not only did he join a band, they got jealous and booted him. Get out. That's exactly what they said.
jokes.setup: Pierce telling a story about someone getting kicked out of a band
jokes.punchline: The misdirection that 'Get out' was literally what the band said
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection joke with good timing. Pierce's delivery would sell this well.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: So this guy gets kicked out of the band, right? And the lead singer says, 'You need to get out.' So the guy's like, 'What do you mean?' And he says, 'Get out.' And the guy's confused, thinking it's some profound statement about life. But no—they literally just wanted him to leave the room.

jokes.text: My...Girlfriend... Is pregnant. Pierce said you were gay. He-- yep, as the sunrise. And I'm deeply ashamed.
jokes.setup: Jeff making up an excuse to leave
jokes.punchline: Having to double down on the gay lie and add shame
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's quick thinking under pressure and willingness to lie.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I can't stay, I have to... I have a thing with my... boyfriend.

dialogue.speaker: Amber
dialogue.line: Your boyfriend?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yes, my boyfriend. His name is... Marcus. And he's very sick. With... gay stuff.

jokes.text: Yeah, my mom told me there'd be white people that did this. With pocket watches, and coffee grinders, and pretending to be into steam boats.
jokes.setup: Britta romanticizing his grandmother's old-fashioned discipline
jokes.punchline: Troy's mom's prediction about white people fetishizing antiquated things
jokes.explanation: Excellent social commentary delivered through Troy's innocent perspective. The specific examples make it funnier and more believable.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Your grandmother sounds amazing. That kind of old-fashioned discipline and structure... there's something so pure about it.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: My mom said white people would eventually fetishize that stuff.

jokes.text: Smiling...Sideways vagina. No. Happy...Sideways vagina. No. Happy face. And that's a clam? 'As happy as a clam.' Yes! 'As happy as a clam'? What a-- Gay guess. Idiot.
jokes.setup: Pictionary game with Pierce drawing terribly
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate guesses followed by calling Jeff's correct answer gay
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - inappropriate and nonsensical while being insulting. The escalation works for the character.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's a giraffe. No wait, a spaceship. Actually, it's a penis.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's a house, Pierce.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well that's gay.

jokes.text: This isn't gonna stop until Pictionary bans the word 'windmill.'
jokes.setup: Police investigating what appears to be a serious Pictionary-related incident
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific solution to prevent future violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - treating board game disputes with the seriousness of public policy. The specificity of 'windmill' makes it even better.

dialogue.speaker: Cop
dialogue.line: So we're here investigating what appears to be a serious Pictionary-related incident.

dialogue.speaker: Cop
dialogue.line: After reviewing the evidence, we've determined the ridiculously specific solution to prevent future violence: no more drawing circles.

jokes.text: Drop your pants and get over my lap.
jokes.setup: Nana about to discipline Britta with a switch
jokes.punchline: The graphic physical instruction
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation of the discipline scenario. The directness creates the cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Nana
dialogue.line: You need to learn respect, young lady. This is going to hurt me more than it hurts you.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, what are you doing with that switch?

dialogue.speaker: Nana
dialogue.line: I'm going to spank you with it, like my mother did to me.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Oh my God, no, no, no! I'm 23 years old!

dialogue.speaker: Nana
dialogue.line: And you'll always be a child to me.

jokes.text: All you have to do is walk away.
jokes.setup: Britta about to submit to corporal punishment from his grandmother
jokes.punchline: Troy's whispered advice highlighting the obvious solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Troy pointing out the absurdity of Britta's willing participation in her own punishment.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Just tell her you're adopted.

jokes.text: I don't understand you, Britta. I don't understand you at all.
jokes.setup: Watching Britta get spanked by his grandmother
jokes.punchline: Troy's genuine confusion and emotional response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's genuine emotional confusion at Britta's behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why is your grandmother spanking you?

jokes.text: Well, it helps that he's kind of desperate and stupid.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking how she gets away with taking Pierce's money
jokes.punchline: Amber's blunt assessment of Pierce's vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment revealing Amber's calculating nature and Pierce's vulnerabilities.

jokes.text: You're gross! You did it with her? Yeah, but not twice. I wanted to do it twice.
jokes.setup: Annie's disgust at Jeff sleeping with Amber
jokes.punchline: Jeff's defense that highlights his failure
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff character moment - his attempt to defend himself only makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: How could you sleep with Amber? She's terrible!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I know she's not great, but I was going through something, and she was... there.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's your defense? That she had the basic ability to exist in the same room as you?

jokes.text: You're gross! You did it with her? Yeah, but not twice. I wanted to do it twice.
jokes.setup: Annie realizes Jeff slept with Pierce's stepdaughter
jokes.punchline: Jeff's defense makes it worse by admitting he wanted to do it again
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's inability to lie well when caught. The 'not twice' defense actually makes him look worse, which is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You slept with Pierce's stepdaughter?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It was one time.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I wanted to do it again, but I didn't.

jokes.text: You're faster than some bacteria.
jokes.setup: Annie criticizing Jeff for sleeping with someone he just met
jokes.punchline: Comparing his speed to microorganisms
jokes.explanation: Clever insult with scientific precision. Great economy and originality in the comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, how can you sleep with someone you just met? That's so irresponsible!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, I'm efficient.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Efficient? You reproduce faster than bacteria!

jokes.text: You really suck, you know that?
jokes.setup: Jeff's moral crisis about Pierce and Amber
jokes.punchline: Annie's blunt assessment of his emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment. Annie cutting through Jeff's self-justification.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Jeff, you're being emotionally manipulative and you know it.

jokes.text: Ding dongs are my favorite.
jokes.setup: After all the harsh discipline and conflict
jokes.punchline: Sweet grandmother moment about enjoying simple treats
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing grandmother's softer side after establishing her as harsh disciplinarian.

dialogue.speaker: Nana Barnes
dialogue.line: You know, after all these years of fighting and discipline, the simple things matter most. Like sitting with family and enjoying a treat together.

jokes.text: I said you were a monster, ma'am. And you said Troy was right. Get me a switch.
jokes.setup: Nana asking what Britta said
jokes.punchline: Britta's honest repetition leading to more punishment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Britta's honesty gets her in more trouble, continuing the discipline theme.

dialogue.speaker: Nana
dialogue.line: What did you say to me?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I said you're a selfish old bat who only thinks about herself!

dialogue.speaker: Nana
dialogue.line: That's what I thought you said.

jokes.text: Yeah. I'll take the 25 grand. Over him, I'd take 25 cents.
jokes.setup: Jeff giving Amber an ultimatum about Pierce
jokes.punchline: Her callous dismissal of any relationship value
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment showing Amber's complete lack of sentiment about Pierce.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, you need to choose: Pierce or me. I'm not sharing you with someone like that.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Jeff, Pierce is my friend. I'm not abandoning him just because you have a problem with him.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Fine. Then we're done.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Okay. I'll see you around, I guess.

jokes.text: Who did you call last week after you farted on Vaughn? [laughing] You. And who did Abed call after that squirrel stole his hot dog? Me. That's sharing your life. If you have friends, you have family.
jokes.setup: Pierce feeling lonely about not having family
jokes.punchline: Jeff's examples of their friendship being mundane but meaningful
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with good specific examples. The mundane nature of the examples makes it more genuine and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know, Jeff, sometimes I feel like I don't have real family.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, we're your family. Remember when we spent three hours arguing about whether a hot dog is a sandwich?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Or that time you fell asleep on my shoulder during a movie and I didn't move for forty minutes because I didn't want to wake you?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's what family does. We show up. We're here.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Really.

jokes.text: You know, if the roles were reversed, I would've had sex with your ex-stepdaughter. I did.
jokes.setup: Pierce making a hypothetical statement about loyalty
jokes.punchline: Jeff's admission that Pierce is actually right
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character consistency. Pierce's hypothetical turns out to be accurate.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: If a friend asked you to help them move a body, you'd do it, right? That's loyalty.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's... okay, you're actually right. I would do that for you.

jokes.text: I hate Glee. I'm not crazy about Glee either. [sobbing] I hate it. I don't understand the appeal at all.
jokes.setup: Jeff crying about his breakup and TV preferences
jokes.punchline: Both men bonding over their shared hatred of Glee
jokes.explanation: Decent bonding moment over shared cultural disdain. The escalating hatred works for the characters.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I can't believe she left me. And now I have to watch Glee alone.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Glee? That show is terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Right? Thank you! Everyone loves it but it's awful.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: The singing is pitchy, the characters are insufferable, and don't get me started on the writing.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Exactly! I thought I was crazy for hating it.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You're not crazy. That show is a crime against television.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what? This breakup might be the best thing that ever happened to me.

jokes.text: Troy? I think I hit G-13.
jokes.setup: Elaborate plan to use vending machine to retrieve phone
jokes.punchline: Abed's understated announcement of plan failure
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff with Abed's characteristic deadpan delivery of bad news.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The phone is in the vending machine.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We can get it out!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I've run the calculations. It won't work.

tmdb_overview: As Family Day lands Jeff in the middle of an affair with Pierce's sexy ex-stepdaughter, Shirley's unruly sons help liberate Abed's Afghani cousin.
insight: Pierce's racism reaches peak absurdity with 50 jokes crammed into genealogy ancestry chaos.
